specific_location: Along CR 8B about a mile south of CR 8 - Owl Creek Road
description: This large tanager was dull yellow underneath from the throat to the undertail coverts and yellowish-olive on most upperparts. It looked noticeably larger than the Western Tanagers it was with in a loose flock. The wings and back were more olive and there was no sign of any wingbars. The grayish bill was heavy and relatively long for a tanager. The crown was olive-yellow, similar to the back, and the face was also olive-yellow though slightly lighter than the crown. There appeared to be darker eye line, but the distance was too great to be sure.Behaviors: This bird was foraging in cottonwood trees along side a dirt road in a riparian area. There was a stream close by the roadway.Call: Not heardPlumage: Adult
